raw_consumer_statement:
Dispute ID at AMEX : XXXX Amount : XXXX XXXX ordered a pair of shoes from XXXX XXXX XXXX I tried them on and realized they were too small. I dropped them off at XXXX the XXXX  day. However XXXX XXXX refused my return because : XXXX. The shoe tip were damaged, and they provided a picture before shipping, taken from the top of the shoes ( attached ). However, the damage was at the front of the shoe tip which couldn't be seen from their picture. To prove that, when they declined my return, and sent the damaged shoes back to me, I took pictures from the same angle ( pictures attached ) and the damage couldn't be seen. So the picture they provided can't prove the shoes were in perfect condition before shipping to me, hence they couldn't use that as an evidence to show that I was the one who caused the damage. 
XXXX. They also claimed that I removed the protective sheets from the shoes. To prove that, they simply provided pictures of shoes without protective sheets and claimed these were from my returned package. Since I am sure that I didn't remove anything from the shoes, I asked them to provide video proof ( or anything that can prove the shoes taken from my returned package were without protective sheets ). But they kept ignoring my request. I refused to acknowledge that those shoes were the ones I returned, and it's also possible that someone removed the protective sheets when they detected damage on the shoes so they can ask the customer to pay for their mistake. 

I disputed the charge on AMEX and provided all the details, proofs and reasons ( attached ). But they kept declining my dispute and passing me the same email from the company. The XXXX  time they declined my dispute soon after I resubmitted the dispute. They are not listening to their customers and not protecting my right. As a XXXX AMEX customer I'm extremely unsastisfied with the service.
